competencies.competency_text: Describes relationships (including between humans and animals/nature) and traditions (art forms, celebrations, festivals) in the family and community
competencies.justification: Bela describes family members, their relationships, and the family pet Shiru across 'Playing Together', 'Laughing Together', and 'Helping Each Other'. Traditions are present through Dadiji's rain songs (oral folk tradition), rangoli with natural materials (IKS craft), and traditional games — all described by students in Write and Draw tasks.

competencies.competency_text: Explains the functioning of local institutions (family, school, bank/post office, market, and panchayat) in different forms (story, drawing, tabulating data, reports), and analyses their roles
competencies.justification: The family is the local institution studied throughout. Students explain its functioning through multiple forms: tables (family members, relatives, work distribution), drawings (family tree Activity 3, family diagram Activity 4), and written descriptions (Write tasks on who does what work, how members help each other).

competencies.competency_text: Uses local materials to create simple objects (family tree, envelopes, origami animals) on their own for display or use in classroom processes
competencies.justification: Activity 2 explicitly directs students to collect fallen leaves and flowers to create a rangoli — a locally-sourced material craft. Activity 3 produces a family-tree diagram and Activity 4 a family drawing, both for classroom display.

competencies.competency_text: Observes and describes cultural diversity in their immediate environment (food, clothing, games, different seasons, festivals related to harvest and sowing)
competencies.justification: Traditional games chhupan-chhupai, peek-a-boo, antakshari, and pitthoo are described and played across 'Laughing Together' and 'Fun with Family and Friends'. Dadiji's rain songs and the rangoli-making tradition appear in 'Singing Together' and 'Helping Each Other'. Find out task asks students to discover grandparents' games — extending cultural observation across generations.

competencies.competency_text: Performs simple inquiry related to specific questions independently or in groups
competencies.justification: Three structured inquiry tasks: Find out what games grandparents played (p.7); Find out who does what work in your family and fill the table (p.15); Let us reflect Discuss on care beyond the family (p.18). Each requires the student to gather information from people in their environment.

competencies.competency_text: Presents observations and findings through different creative modes (drawing, diagram, poem, play, skit, oral and written expression)
competencies.justification: Multiple creative output modes: Activity 2 (rangoli/draw), Activity 3 (family-relationship diagram), Activity 4 (family drawing), Let us reflect Draw (happy family moments), Let us reflect Write (written responses), Discuss tasks (oral expression), and the word-search activity.
